options.content:
## Kiro Credits + DORA Correlation
This dashboard correlates **Kiro AI usage (credits and messages)** with **DORA** performance indicators at a weekly aggregate level.

- **Pearson's r** measures linear correlation: negative r suggests higher AI usage may correlate with shorter cycle times.
- Data is aggregated by **week** and joined on `week_start`.

panels.description: Pearson correlation coefficient between weekly Kiro credits and PR cycle time

targets.rawSql:
WITH _kiro_weekly AS (
  SELECT
    DATE_SUB(DATE(date), INTERVAL WEEKDAY(DATE(date)) DAY) AS week_start,
    SUM(credits_used) AS weekly_credits
  FROM _tool_q_dev_user_report
  WHERE $__timeFilter(date)
  GROUP BY DATE_SUB(DATE(date), INTERVAL WEEKDAY(DATE(date)) DAY)
),
_pr_weekly AS (
  SELECT
    DATE_SUB(DATE(pr_merged_date), INTERVAL WEEKDAY(DATE(pr_merged_date)) DAY) AS week_start,
    AVG(pr_cycle_time) / 60.0 AS avg_cycle_hours
  FROM project_pr_metrics
  WHERE pr_merged_date IS NOT NULL
    AND pr_cycle_time IS NOT NULL
    AND $__timeFilter(pr_merged_date)
  GROUP BY DATE_SUB(DATE(pr_merged_date), INTERVAL WEEKDAY(DATE(pr_merged_date)) DAY)
),
_joined AS (
  SELECT k.weekly_credits AS x, p.avg_cycle_hours AS y
  FROM _kiro_weekly k
  INNER JOIN _pr_weekly p ON k.week_start = p.week_start
),
_stats AS (
  SELECT COUNT(*) AS n, AVG(x) AS mx, AVG(y) AS my, STDDEV_POP(x) AS sx, STDDEV_POP(y) AS sy
  FROM _joined
)
SELECT CASE
  WHEN s.n < 4 THEN NULL
  WHEN s.sx = 0 OR s.sy = 0 THEN 0
  ELSE ROUND((SELECT SUM((j.x - s.mx) * (j.y - s.my)) FROM _joined j) / (s.n * s.sx * s.sy), 2)
END AS 'r'
FROM _stats s

panels.title: Credits vs Cycle Time (r)

panels.description: Total Kiro credits consumed in period

targets.rawSql:
SELECT SUM(credits_used) AS 'Credits Used'
FROM _tool_q_dev_user_report
WHERE $__timeFilter(date)

panels.title: Total Credits

panels.description: Median PR cycle time in hours

targets.rawSql:
WITH _ranked AS (
  SELECT pr_cycle_time / 60.0 AS ct,
    PERCENT_RANK() OVER (ORDER BY pr_cycle_time) AS prank
  FROM project_pr_metrics
  WHERE pr_merged_date IS NOT NULL AND pr_cycle_time IS NOT NULL
    AND $__timeFilter(pr_merged_date)
)
SELECT ROUND(MAX(ct), 1) AS 'Median Cycle Time'
FROM _ranked WHERE prank <= 0.5

panels.title: Median Cycle Time

panels.description: Cycle time comparison: weeks with above-median vs below-median AI credits usage

targets.rawSql:
WITH _kiro_weekly AS (
  SELECT DATE_SUB(DATE(date), INTERVAL WEEKDAY(DATE(date)) DAY) AS week_start,
    SUM(credits_used) AS weekly_credits
  FROM _tool_q_dev_user_report WHERE $__timeFilter(date)
  GROUP BY DATE_SUB(DATE(date), INTERVAL WEEKDAY(DATE(date)) DAY)
),
_pr_weekly AS (
  SELECT DATE_SUB(DATE(pr_merged_date), INTERVAL WEEKDAY(DATE(pr_merged_date)) DAY) AS week_start,
    AVG(pr_cycle_time) / 60.0 AS avg_ct
  FROM project_pr_metrics
  WHERE pr_merged_date IS NOT NULL AND pr_cycle_time IS NOT NULL AND $__timeFilter(pr_merged_date)
  GROUP BY DATE_SUB(DATE(pr_merged_date), INTERVAL WEEKDAY(DATE(pr_merged_date)) DAY)
),
_joined AS (
  SELECT k.weekly_credits, p.avg_ct FROM _kiro_weekly k INNER JOIN _pr_weekly p ON k.week_start = p.week_start
),
_med AS (
  SELECT weekly_credits AS med FROM _joined ORDER BY weekly_credits LIMIT 1 OFFSET (SELECT FLOOR(COUNT(*)/2) FROM _joined)
)
SELECT
  CASE WHEN j.weekly_credits >= m.med THEN 'High AI Usage' ELSE 'Low AI Usage' END AS 'Tier',
  ROUND(AVG(j.avg_ct), 1) AS 'Avg Cycle Time'
FROM _joined j, _med m
GROUP BY CASE WHEN j.weekly_credits >= m.med THEN 'High AI Usage' ELSE 'Low AI Usage' END

panels.title: Cycle Time: High vs Low AI Usage

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
  DATE_SUB(DATE(date), INTERVAL WEEKDAY(DATE(date)) DAY) AS time,
  SUM(credits_used) AS 'Credits Used',
  COUNT(DISTINCT user_id) AS 'Active Users'
FROM _tool_q_dev_user_report
WHERE $__timeFilter(date)
GROUP BY DATE_SUB(DATE(date), INTERVAL WEEKDAY(DATE(date)) DAY)
ORDER BY time

panels.description: Weekly average PR cycle time in hours

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
  DATE_SUB(DATE(pr_merged_date), INTERVAL WEEKDAY(DATE(pr_merged_date)) DAY) AS time,
  ROUND(AVG(pr_cycle_time) / 60.0, 1) AS 'Avg Cycle Time (hrs)',
  COUNT(*) AS 'PRs Merged'
FROM project_pr_metrics
WHERE pr_merged_date IS NOT NULL AND pr_cycle_time IS NOT NULL
  AND $__timeFilter(pr_merged_date)
GROUP BY DATE_SUB(DATE(pr_merged_date), INTERVAL WEEKDAY(DATE(pr_merged_date)) DAY)
ORDER BY time

panels.title: Weekly PR Cycle Time & Volume

panels.description: Pearson correlation between weekly credits and deployment count

targets.rawSql:
WITH _kiro_weekly AS (
  SELECT DATE_SUB(DATE(date), INTERVAL WEEKDAY(DATE(date)) DAY) AS week_start,
    SUM(credits_used) AS weekly_credits
  FROM _tool_q_dev_user_report WHERE $__timeFilter(date)
  GROUP BY DATE_SUB(DATE(date), INTERVAL WEEKDAY(DATE(date)) DAY)
),
_deploy_weekly AS (
  SELECT DATE_SUB(DATE(cdc.finished_date), INTERVAL WEEKDAY(DATE(cdc.finished_date)) DAY) AS week_start,
    COUNT(DISTINCT cdc.cicd_deployment_id) AS deploys
  FROM cicd_deployment_commits cdc
  JOIN project_mapping pm ON cdc.cicd_scope_id = pm.row_id AND pm.`table` = 'cicd_scopes'
  WHERE cdc.result = 'SUCCESS' AND cdc.environment = 'PRODUCTION'
    AND $__timeFilter(cdc.finished_date)
  GROUP BY DATE_SUB(DATE(cdc.finished_date), INTERVAL WEEKDAY(DATE(cdc.finished_date)) DAY)
),
_joined AS (
  SELECT k.weekly_credits AS x, d.deploys AS y
  FROM _kiro_weekly k INNER JOIN _deploy_weekly d ON k.week_start = d.week_start
),
_stats AS (
  SELECT COUNT(*) AS n, AVG(x) AS mx, AVG(y) AS my, STDDEV_POP(x) AS sx, STDDEV_POP(y) AS sy FROM _joined
)
SELECT CASE
  WHEN s.n < 4 THEN NULL
  WHEN s.sx = 0 OR s.sy = 0 THEN 0
  ELSE ROUND((SELECT SUM((j.x - s.mx) * (j.y - s.my)) FROM _joined j) / (s.n * s.sx * s.sy), 2)
END AS 'r'
FROM _stats s

panels.title: Credits vs Deploy Freq (r)

panels.description: Weekly credits overlaid with deployment count

targets.rawSql:
SELECT time, SUM(credits) AS 'Credits Used', SUM(deploys) AS 'Deployments'
FROM (
  SELECT DATE_SUB(DATE(date), INTERVAL WEEKDAY(DATE(date)) DAY) AS time,
    SUM(credits_used) AS credits, 0 AS deploys
  FROM _tool_q_dev_user_report WHERE $__timeFilter(date)
  GROUP BY DATE_SUB(DATE(date), INTERVAL WEEKDAY(DATE(date)) DAY)
  UNION ALL
  SELECT DATE_SUB(DATE(cdc.finished_date), INTERVAL WEEKDAY(DATE(cdc.finished_date)) DAY) AS time,
    0 AS credits, COUNT(DISTINCT cdc.cicd_deployment_id) AS deploys
  FROM cicd_deployment_commits cdc
  JOIN project_mapping pm ON cdc.cicd_scope_id = pm.row_id AND pm.`table` = 'cicd_scopes'
  WHERE cdc.result = 'SUCCESS' AND cdc.environment = 'PRODUCTION'
    AND $__timeFilter(cdc.finished_date)
  GROUP BY DATE_SUB(DATE(cdc.finished_date), INTERVAL WEEKDAY(DATE(cdc.finished_date)) DAY)
) combined
GROUP BY time ORDER BY time

panels.title: Weekly Credits vs Deployments

panels.description: Change failure rate: % of deployments that caused incidents

targets.rawSql:
SELECT time, SUM(credits) AS 'Credits Used', SUM(cfr) AS 'Change Failure Rate'
FROM (
  SELECT DATE_SUB(DATE(date), INTERVAL WEEKDAY(DATE(date)) DAY) AS time,
    SUM(credits_used) AS credits, 0 AS cfr
  FROM _tool_q_dev_user_report WHERE $__timeFilter(date)
  GROUP BY DATE_SUB(DATE(date), INTERVAL WEEKDAY(DATE(date)) DAY)
  UNION ALL
  SELECT DATE_SUB(DATE(d.deployment_finished_date), INTERVAL WEEKDAY(DATE(d.deployment_finished_date)) DAY) AS time,
    0 AS credits,
    SUM(CASE WHEN i.id IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / COUNT(DISTINCT d.deployment_id) AS cfr
  FROM (
    SELECT cdc.cicd_deployment_id AS deployment_id, MAX(cdc.finished_date) AS deployment_finished_date
    FROM cicd_deployment_commits cdc
    JOIN project_mapping pm ON cdc.cicd_scope_id = pm.row_id AND pm.`table` = 'cicd_scopes'
    WHERE cdc.result = 'SUCCESS' AND cdc.environment = 'PRODUCTION'
      AND $__timeFilter(cdc.finished_date)
    GROUP BY cdc.cicd_deployment_id
  ) d
  LEFT JOIN project_incident_deployment_relationships pidr ON d.deployment_id = pidr.deployment_id
  LEFT JOIN incidents i ON pidr.id = i.id
  GROUP BY DATE_SUB(DATE(d.deployment_finished_date), INTERVAL WEEKDAY(DATE(d.deployment_finished_date)) DAY)
) combined
GROUP BY time ORDER BY time

panels.title: Weekly Credits vs Change Failure Rate
